title: SceneRoll: Stable Unbounded 3D Gaussian Scene Generation via Masked Diffusion Forcing
teaser: Replace the causal transformer of GaussianGPT with a masked diffusion-forcing model over its discrete 3D Gaussian scene tokens: per-token corruption levels enable arbitrary-region scene completion, stabler unbounded rollout, and uncertainty-aware conditioning on partial real-world scans.
abstract: GaussianGPT tokenizes 3D Gaussian Splatting scenes into a discrete latent grid (a sparse-convolutional autoencoder with lookup-free quantization) and generates 4m x 4m scene chunks with a causal transformer over xyz-serialized tokens. The autoregressive formulation, however, restricts conditioning to serialization-order prefixes, and generation quality degrades with distance from the seed region during unbounded outpainting — markedly worse along the serialization axis than across it. Diffusion forcing offers a principled alternative: every token carries an independent corruption level, causality is controlled through noise rather than attention masks, and any subset of tokens — even unreliable ones — can serve as conditioning. In this project, we keep GaussianGPT's released, pretrained scene tokenizer and replace its causal transformer with a full-attention model trained under a masked (discrete) diffusion-forcing objective, following recent discrete analogues such as MaskFlow and AR-DF. Our hypotheses: (i) arbitrary-region scene completion falls out of the training objective, in a setting where prefix-based autoregression structurally cannot compete; (ii) rolling out with partially-corrupted self-generated context stabilizes unbounded scene generation and removes the axis-dependent degradation; (iii) mapping real-scan confidence to per-token corruption levels enables uncertainty-aware completion of partial ScanNet++ scans — an open problem explicitly named by the original paper. As a bonus, parallel masked decoding should cut per-chunk generation time well below sequential autoregressive sampling.
milestones: Literature review (diffusion forcing and its transformer variant, discrete analogues MaskFlow / AR-DF / block diffusion, chunk-based 3D scene generation such as L3DG and NuiScene); set up the released GaussianGPT codebase, reproduce autoregressive sampling on 3D-FRONT, and measure the frozen tokenizer's reconstruction ceiling (render-space PSNR/LPIPS of encode-decode), which upper-bounds everything downstream.

Train a full-attention transformer (reusing GaussianGPT's 3D RoPE) with a masked diffusion-forcing objective on feature tokens over ground-truth structure for single scene chunks, using region-structured rather than i.i.d. per-token masking; match the autoregressive baseline on FID/KID and compare sampling speed.

Extend the objective to scene structure (occupancy as categorical states on the dense latent grid, or interleaved position tokens); evaluate arbitrary-region completion against the prefix-only autoregressive baseline and L3DG with RePaint, using the original completion protocol plus new non-prefix region layouts.

Unbounded scene rollout with sliding windows and partially-corrupted self-generated context; reproduce the KID-per-tile degradation analysis of GaussianGPT and test whether the axis-dependent quality falloff disappears.
(Stretch) Uncertainty-aware real-scan completion: map per-region scan confidence to per-token corruption levels and evaluate scene completion on partial ScanNet++ scans against completion baselines.

prerequisites.label: Advanced Deep Learning for Computer Vision: Visual Computing (ADL4CV)

references.label: GaussianGPT: Towards Autoregressive 3D Gaussian Scene Generation
Diffusion Forcing: Next-token Prediction Meets Full-Sequence Diffusion
MaskFlow: Discrete Flows for Flexible and Efficient Long Video Generation
Lumos-1: On Autoregressive Video Generation with Discrete Diffusion from a Unified Model Perspective
